title: Reasoning Summary
content: The system has completed the automated analysis. MRI shows suspicious lymph nodes in the left external iliac and obturator regions. Combined with imaging features, this suggests a relatively high risk of pelvic lymph node metastasis. Evidence for parametrial invasion is relatively insufficient, and the current assessment should be further confirmed with contrast-enhanced imaging and clinical history.

title: Assessment Conclusion
content: Current evidence suggests a high risk of pelvic lymph node metastasis and a low risk of parametrial invasion.

title: Risk Warning
content: PET/CT and complete clinical history are missing. Manual review of the high-risk conclusion is recommended.

title: Next-Step Recommendations
items: Consider PET/CT to further evaluate lymph node status.
Recommend completing the detailed clinical history.
After additional imaging data are available, recommend further treatment planning through MDT discussion.
